question: Describe the primary function of the Control Unit (CU) within a CPU and explain how it directs the operation of a computer.
expectedPoints: The Control Unit (CU) is a component of the CPU that directs the operation of the processor.
It interprets the instructions of a program stored in memory and generates the timing and control signals necessary for their execution.
It coordinates the activities of the other hardware components (ALU, registers, memory, I/O) by sending control signals to them.

question: Explain the von Neumann architecture, listing its main components and describing the role of the stored-program concept.
expectedPoints: The von Neumann architecture is characterized by a central processing unit (CPU), a memory unit, and input/output mechanisms.
Its main components are: the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), the Control Unit (CU), memory, and I/O devices, all connected by a system bus.
The stored-program concept is fundamental, meaning that both program instructions and data are stored together in the same memory.
This allows the computer to be easily reprogrammed without rewiring hardware, as the CPU fetches instructions sequentially from memory for execution.

question: Trace and explain the complete fetch-decode-execute cycle for a single machine instruction, detailing the role of the major CPU components (Program Counter, Memory Address Register, Memory Data Register, Instruction Register, Control Unit, and ALU) at each stage.
expectedPoints: Fetch Stage: The address of the next instruction is held in the Program Counter (PC). This address is copied to the Memory Address Register (MAR). A memory read operation is initiated. The instruction is fetched from memory and placed into the Memory Data Register (MDR), then transferred to the Instruction Register (IR). The PC is incremented to point to the next instruction.
Decode Stage: The Control Unit (CU) decodes the instruction in the IR to determine the operation (opcode) and the operands (if any).

Execute Stage: The CU generates the necessary control signals to perform the operation. This may involve: transferring data from registers to the ALU, performing a calculation in the ALU, storing a result back to a register or memory, or performing an I/O operation. The specific actions depend entirely on the decoded instruction.
